channel.title: Awesome Search KG
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Awesome Search KG

item.title: Doug Turnbull
item.description: Doug Turnbull Co-founder of OpenSource Connections (OSC); co-author of Relevant Search (O’Reilly).

item.title: All about Information Retrieval & Search
item.description: Awesome Search — Information Retrieval & Search Knowledge Graph Hello, I am Andrew.

item.title: History
item.description: Awesome Search — Note History Chronological log of notes added to this knowledge graph. Newest first.

item.title: Agentic Search
item.description: Agentic Search Definition Agentic search refers to search systems that go beyond simple retrieval to plan, decompose, execute, verify, and refine — functioning more like engineering teams pursuing goals under uncertainty than simple lookup functions.

item.title: RAG
item.description: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) Definition RAG combines a retrieval system with a generative language model.

item.title: Conversational and Agentic Search
item.description: Conversational and Agentic Search Search is shifting from single-turn keyword queries toward multi-turn dialogue and autonomous agent-driven retrieval.

item.title: index
item.description: Awesome Search — Information Retrieval & Search Knowledge Graph Hello, I am Andrew.

item.title: Elasticsearch vs. OpenSearch (2025): The Definitive Showdown
item.description: Elasticsearch vs. OpenSearch (2025): The Definitive Showdown Summary An Elasticsearch-favorable comparison of the two platforms in 2025.

item.title: From RAG to Search-R1 - Evolving Language Models from Knowledge Retrieval to Autonomous Reasoning
item.description: From RAG to Search-R1: Evolving Language Models from Knowledge Retrieval to Autonomous Reasoning An accessible comparison of RAG and Search-R1 — tracing the evolution from single-pass retrieval toward autonomous, multi-turn search-and-reasoning agents trained with reinforcement learning.

item.title: Observability for AI Workloads: A New Paradigm for a New Era
item.description: Observability for AI Workloads: A New Paradigm for a New Era Summary A 2026 retrospective on how AI workloads changed observability requirements.
